label: Beowulf

sections.label: Beowulf
sections.original:
Versions of
Beowulf
include:
Versions in Old English (Anglo-Saxon)
Beowulf (Zupitza)
, Autotypes (facsmilies) of the MS, with transliteration and notes by
Julius Zupitza
(1882).
IA
Beowulf (Harrison and Sharp)
, edited by
James A. Harrison
and
Robert Sharp
(1883); based on Moritz Heyne’s fourth edition of 1879; but without Heyne’s (German) notes.
IA
Beowulf (Wyatt)
, edited by
A. J. Wyatt
(1894); based on Zupitza’s autotypes.
Translations into modern English
Beowulf (Wackerbarth)
, translated by
Athanasius Diedrich Wackerbarth
(1849)
IA
Beowulf (Hall)
, translated by Lesslie Hall (1892), based on the Heyne-Socin text. (
Project Gutenberg text
)
The Tale of Beowulf
, translated by
and
A. J. Wyatt
, revised edition (1898); with classical  rhyme-pattern.
Beowulf
translated by
Francis B. Gummere
(1909) in
The Oldest English Epic
; maintaining the Anglo-Saxon metre and rhyme-pattern.
Adaptations
"
The Story of Beowulf, Grendel, and Grendel's Mother
" and "
The Story of Beowulf and the Fire Drake
". H. S. C. Everard; H. J. Ford (illustr.). The Red Book of Animal Stories. Lang (ed.)  London: Longman. 1899
"
Beowulf
". Jean Lang; Helen Stratton (illustr.). A Book of Myths. 1914
Works about
"
Beowulf
," by
Henry Bradley
in
Encyclopædia Britannica
(11th ed., 1911)

volumeTitle: Anglo-Saxon Riddles of the Exeter Book
